# Byzantine Chant Notation Assistant

This is a simple tool to help with the transcription of Byzantine neume sequences to Western staff pitches. It is a work in progress, and is not yet ready for use.

## Usage

To use this tool, you need to have Python 3.6 or later installed on your system. You can then run the tool from the command line by typing `python main.py`.

When you run the tool, you will be prompted to enter the mode of the chant, the neume sequence, and the staff pitches. The mode is a number from 1 to 8, and the neume sequence is a string of neume names separated by spaces. The staff pitches are a string of

G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4 G4
